title: <div><tt>2. Core Mechanism — how the Nexus propagates, mutates, or annihilates p
content:
2. Core Mechanism — how the Nexus propagates, mutates, or annihilates purely through linguistic means.
3. Inhabited by Whom? — the "speakers" or "readers" (if such categories still exist) and their linguistic condition.
4. Daily Linguistic Life — what it feels like to think, love, lie, or die inside this ontology.
5. One fully invented script or notational system (show 8–15 glyphs/symbols with exact linguistic function and example usage).
6. The Inherent Catastrophe — the precise linguistic contradiction that will unravel this Nexus.
7. Final utterance — a short text (100–200 words) written entirely in the rules of this ontology that makes the reader’s own language feel temporarily foreign.

Word count: 1000–1400. Be cold, precise, surgical. Never use metaphors that imply physicality (no "towering," no "weaving," no "birth"). Use only the violence and caress of pure text.

Linguistic Mutation Seed for this query: [INSERT 1–10 HERE]
—
context: From document: You are a visionary designer tasked with creating the ultimate synthesis… — chunk 10 (chunk 10/18)
